mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5 despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) signal deteriorating labor market conditions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of periods where PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in > 40% of periods with INDPRO growth exceeding 1.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction (HOUST) indicates strong housing market demand, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in > 50% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1000 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive quarterly declines in GDP (GDPC1) signal economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through reduced corporate profitability expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 40% of periods with GDPC1 declining for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust consumer and business demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are negative in > 50% of periods where RSAFS and HOUST both rise by > 2% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A 200-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) within a 3-month window signals aggressive monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by 200 bps over 3 months.
